patches.find: confirmed by ships (Soviet Ob expedition 1956–1957; HMS Endurance patrols; modern Southern Ocean yacht races) and by satellite-tracked vessel routes that log distance by time × speed, fuel consumption, and GPS. This is a factor-of-eight discrepancy.
patches.replace: confirmed by satellite-tracked circumnavigation records (Jules Verne Trophy and Vendée Globe routes log distance by elapsed time, speed, fuel consumption, and continuous GPS) and by Antarctic research-vessel deployments. This is a factor-of-eight discrepancy at the 70°S coast (dome predicts ~112,000 km vs. measured ~13,700 km, ratio ≈ 8.2×); the full rim figure (125,760 km) gives a factor of nine.
patches.rationale: ISS-971: Soviet Ob was NOT a full Antarctic circumnavigator (citation failure flagged by curmudgeon). HMS Endurance patrols were not circumnavigations. Modern Southern Ocean racing records (Vendée Globe, Jules Verne Trophy) do circumnavigate and are better supported. Also fixes the factor-of-eight/nine inconsistency: body now clarifies 8× (70°S) vs 9× (rim) before the conclusion.

patches.find: sailing around it should take nine times longer than it does.
patches.replace: sailing around it should take eight to nine times longer than it does.
patches.rationale: ISS-971: Consistency fix. Body now says 8× (70°S path) and 9× (full rim); conclusion should reflect both. 'Eight to nine times' is internally consistent and slightly conservative.

patches.find: for stars too bright for Gaia (Sirius, Vega, Betelgeuse, Deneb), values are from Hipparcos (van Leeuwen 2007).
patches.replace: for stars too bright for Gaia (Sirius, Vega, Deneb), values are from Hipparcos (van Leeuwen 2007); Betelgeuse parallax is from Harper et al. 2017 (AJ 154, 11), a combined VLA + Hipparcos solution (van Leeuwen 2007 alone gives 6.55 ± 0.83 mas).
patches.rationale: ISS-974: Betelgeuse 4.51 ± 0.80 mas is NOT from van Leeuwen 2007 (which gives 6.55 ± 0.83 mas). Correct source is Harper et al. 2017 (AJ 154, 11). Crediting the wrong paper is a citation failure that a dome defender can use against us.
